channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - AI & Machine Learning
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: AI & Machine Learning

item.title: Delivering living medicines with polysaccharide derivatives for disease treatment.
item.description: Journal of controlled release : official journal of the Controlled Release Society - Review - Living medicines represent a revolutionary approach to modern therapeutics, but their effective delivery and in vivo functional maintenance pose significant challenges. Polysaccharide derivatives h...

item.title: Brief Review: Rethinking Colonic Redundancy in Gastroenterology.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Review - Dolichocolon (DC), or colonic redundancy, is an elongated and tortuous colon described as early as 1820, yet it remains underrecognized in clinical gastroenterology. Advances in imaging and motilit...

item.title: Atopic dermatitis.
item.description: Nature reviews. Disease primers - Review - Atopic dermatitis (AD) is the most common inflammatory skin disease and carries the highest disability-adjusted life-years burden, ranking 15th among all non-fatal diseases globally. It is characte...

item.title: A novel deep learning network for small bowel ulcerative lesion detection and differential diagnosis on double-balloon endoscopy images.
item.description: Biomedical physics & engineering express - Differentiating small bowel ulcerative diseases (SBUDs) on double-balloon endoscopy (DBE) is challenging. We aimed to develop an artificial intelligence (AI) model using DBE images for accurate SBU...

item.title: Learning and deskilling effects of artificial intelligence in colonoscopy among endoscopists with different levels of experience: a pragmatic, prospective trial.
item.description: Endoscopy - Clinical trial - The effect of computer-aided detection (CADe) on performance of endoscopists with different experience levels is not well understood. This study assessed whether CADe use promotes learning or deski...

item.title: Performance of IBD machine learning classifiers varies across microbiome training data independent of geographic diversity.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - Microbiome-based machine learning classifiers show increasing promise for disease identification across gastrointestinal, metabolic, and immune-mediated conditions. Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)...

item.title: Resistant starch selectively depletes a putative pathobiont-enriched gut microbial module: evidence from multiple dietary fiber intervention cohorts.
item.description: Frontiers in nutrition - Dietary fiber, particularly resistant starch (RS), has been proposed to modulate the gut microbiota and immune microenvironment; however, individual intervention studies yield inconsistent results ...
